locations.name: Cardiff-by-the-Sea
locations.description: A Cardiff-by-the-Sea institution serving authentic Mexican food around the clock — breakfast burritos at dawn, late-night tacos, always fresh.
locations.seoTitle: Mexican Food in Cardiff-by-the-Sea — Open 24 Hours | El Pueblo
locations.seoDescription: Mexican food in Cardiff-by-the-Sea, open 24 hours at 820 Birmingham Drive. Fish tacos, breakfast burritos, late-night eats. Family-owned.

sections.h2: Mexican Food in Cardiff-by-the-Sea — Open 24 Hours
paragraphs: El Pueblo's Cardiff-by-the-Sea location is our flagship — the original kitchen that started the neighborhood's love affair with fresh, fast, affordable Mexican food. Tucked just off the 5 at 820 Birmingham Drive, this is the only Mexican restaurant in Cardiff open 24 hours a day, seven days a week. Surfers grab breakfast burritos before dawn. Night shifts drop in for carne asada tacos at 2am. Families roll through after soccer practice. Whenever you're hungry, we're open.

Family-owned, El Pueblo Cardiff has become a neighborhood staple — the kind of place locals send visitors, and the first stop for everyone coming back to town. Our kitchen turns out the same menu you'll find at our Carlsbad, Carmel Valley, and Del Mar locations, all made fresh every day with produce delivered each morning.

sections.h2: Fish Tacos, Breakfast Burritos, and Late-Night Eats in Cardiff
paragraphs: Cardiff is where the famous $1.39 Fish Taco was born — crispy fried-to-order fillet, chipotle sauce, fresh pico, crisp cabbage on a warm corn tortilla, with no limit on how many you can order. It's the taco we've never raised the price on. Locals come in for five at a time.

Beyond the fish taco, the Cardiff kitchen serves the full El Pueblo menu all 24 hours: breakfast burritos (Super, Steak and Eggs, Machaca, Chorizo), huevos rancheros and chilaquiles in the early morning, carne asada plates and burritos through the day, and late-night favorites like adobada tacos, carnitas quesadillas, and carne asada fries for the post-midnight crowd.

sections.h2: Easy In, Easy Out — Right Off the 5 in Cardiff
paragraphs: El Pueblo Cardiff sits at 820 Birmingham Drive, sharing an address with Cardiff Valero — gas, snacks, and cold drinks next door. Fuel up the car and the people in it at the same stop. Order at the counter, take it to go, or eat in the lot with the windows down — it's how Cardiff does it.

faq.q: Are you really open 24 hours?
faq.a: Yes. El Pueblo Cardiff-by-the-Sea is open 24 hours, 7 days a week — the only 24-hour Mexican food in Cardiff. The full menu is available all night, including fish tacos, burritos, and carne asada plates.

faq.q: Where is El Pueblo Cardiff located?
faq.a: 820 Birmingham Drive, Cardiff, CA 92007 — just off the 5 freeway at the Birmingham exit. We share a parking lot with Cardiff Valero gas station.

faq.q: Where do I park?
faq.a: 820 Birmingham Drive shares its lot with Cardiff Valero gas station — plenty of parking, easy in and out. We're a counter-service location; most orders are takeout or eaten in the lot.

faq.q: How much are your fish tacos?
faq.a: $1.39 each, with no limit on how many you can order. Our signature fish taco — crispy fried-to-order fillet, chipotle sauce, fresh pico, cabbage on a warm corn tortilla — and we've never raised the price.

faq.q: Do you serve breakfast at night?
faq.a: Yes. Breakfast burritos, huevos rancheros, chilaquiles, and every other breakfast item on our menu are available 24 hours at the Cardiff location.

locations.name: Carlsbad
locations.description: Our La Costa Town Square location — easy to find after errands or a beach day, with the same fresh Mexican menu you know from El Pueblo.
locations.seoTitle: Mexican Food in Carlsbad — La Costa Town Square | El Pueblo
locations.seoDescription: Mexican food in Carlsbad at La Costa Town Square, 7720 Rancho Santa Fe Road. Fish tacos, breakfast burritos, family plates. Open 6am–10pm weekdays, til midnight Fri/Sat.

sections.h2: Mexican Food in Carlsbad — La Costa Town Square
paragraphs: El Pueblo Carlsbad sits in the heart of La Costa Town Square at 7720 Rancho Santa Fe Road, across from the shops and a short drive from South Carlsbad State Beach, La Costa Resort, and the Omni La Costa. Whether you're rolling in after a morning at the beach, grabbing dinner after errands at the Town Square shops, or feeding the family between kids' sports, our Carlsbad kitchen has you covered.

We're open early (6am for breakfast burritos) and late (until midnight on Friday and Saturday), with a full menu of the Mexican food that's made El Pueblo a North County institution. Counter-service seating inside, a parking lot outside, and the same fresh-every-day kitchen that feeds our other four locations.

sections.h2: Fish Tacos, Breakfast Burritos, and Family-Size Plates
paragraphs: Our Carlsbad menu is the full El Pueblo spread: the signature $1.39 fish taco (fried-to-order, chipotle sauce, cabbage, pico, all day), breakfast burritos from 6am (Super, Steak and Eggs, Machaca, Chorizo, classic Breakfast), enchiladas, carne asada plates, fajita plates, chile rellenos, tortas, quesadillas, and Mexican soups like pozole and chicken tortilla soup.

Feeding a crowd? Our party packs work from our Carlsbad kitchen — make-your-own taco packs, rolled tacos, enchiladas, quesadilla trays, and chips-and-salsa trays for gatherings of any size. Call ahead for catering, or order same-day for pickup.

La Costa families know us as the easy dinner answer — fast enough for a weeknight, cheap enough to feed four kids, and close enough to swing through after Alga Norte Park or Stagecoach. We have a kids menu (kids quesadilla, bean and cheese burrito, rice and beans), horchata and jamaica on tap, and no-fuss counter service so you're never stuck waiting for a check.

faq.q: Where is El Pueblo Carlsbad?
faq.a: 7720 Rancho Santa Fe Road, Carlsbad, CA 92009 — in La Costa Town Square, across from the shops and close to La Costa Resort.

faq.q: What are El Pueblo Carlsbad's hours?
faq.a: Sunday through Thursday we're open 6am to 10pm. Friday and Saturday we stay open until midnight. Breakfast menu starts at 6am daily.

faq.q: Do you have a kids menu?
faq.a: Yes. The Carlsbad location has a kids menu with kids quesadilla, bean and cheese burrito, and sides. Horchata and jamaica are on the drink menu.

faq.q: Is there parking at La Costa Town Square?
faq.a: Yes. La Costa Town Square has a large shared parking lot — no meters, easy access right off Rancho Santa Fe Road.

faq.q: Do you cater in Carlsbad?
faq.a: Yes. Our Carlsbad kitchen fulfills the full El Pueblo catering and party-pack menu — make-your-own taco packs, rolled tacos, enchilada trays, quesadilla trays, and chips-and-salsa trays. Call the store or submit a catering request online.

locations.name: Carmel Valley
locations.description: Carmel Valley's full-bar location — premium tequila, mezcal, craft margaritas, and a heated patio that's open year-round for dinner or private events.
locations.seoTitle: Mexican Food in Carmel Valley, San Diego | El Pueblo — Full Bar
locations.seoDescription: Mexican food in Carmel Valley San Diego at 5965 Village Way. Full bar, heated patio, premium tequila & mezcal, craft margaritas. Open 6am–midnight daily. Private events welcome.

sections.h2: Mexican Food in Carmel Valley, San Diego
paragraphs: El Pueblo Carmel Valley is our San Diego proper location — tucked into the Village Walk shops at 5965 Village Way, minutes from Pacific Highlands Ranch, Del Mar Heights, and the 56. It's where North County meets the city: a neighborhood restaurant with a full bar, a heated patio, and a menu that runs from early breakfast through late-night dinner every day of the year.

Family-owned, this is the location locals call their go-to. The kitchen is open from 6am to midnight, seven days a week. Breakfast burritos at sunrise. Lunch plates at noon. Margaritas on the patio by 5. Tequila flights at 10pm. The rhythm of Carmel Valley, one table at a time.

sections.h2: Full Bar, Heated Patio, and Private Events
paragraphs: Our Carmel Valley bar is stocked with premium tequila, mezcal, and the house margaritas that have become a neighborhood fixture — silver, gold, spicy, skinny, by the glass or the pitcher. We pour tequila flights (blanco, reposado, añejo side-by-side) and we have a short mezcal list for the curious. Our bartenders know the menu cold, so tell them what you want and they'll match it.

The heated patio is the reason people book birthdays, bridal showers, team dinners, and end-of-season parties with us. It's open year-round — heat lamps in winter, shade and ocean breeze the rest of the year. Private events start at 8 guests; we build the catering menu around your group size.

sections.h2: Fish Tacos, Margaritas, and the Full El Pueblo Menu
paragraphs: Carmel Valley serves the full El Pueblo menu — our signature $1.39 fish tacos (fried-to-order, chipotle sauce, cabbage, pico, no limit), breakfast burritos, carne asada plates, enchiladas, chile rellenos, fajitas, quesadillas, and tortas. Pair any of it with a house margarita, a cold Pacifico, or a mezcal pour; our Carmel Valley regulars lean hard on the spicy margarita.

faq.q: Where is El Pueblo Carmel Valley?
faq.a: 5965 Village Way, Suite E107, San Diego, CA 92130 — in the Village Walk shops, near the Del Mar Heights / Carmel Valley Road junction off the 56.

faq.q: Do you have a full bar?
faq.a: Yes. El Pueblo Carmel Valley has a full bar with premium tequila, mezcal, and craft margaritas. Tequila flights available on request.

faq.q: Is the patio heated?
faq.a: Yes. Our heated patio is open year-round and is where most evening dining happens. It also doubles as private-event space for parties of 8 or more.

faq.q: Can I reserve a table?
faq.a: Reservations are only available for private events on the patio (8+ guests). Inside dining is first-come, first-served — weekends fill up fast around 6pm.

faq.q: Are kids welcome?
faq.a: Yes. Carmel Valley is family-friendly — we have a kids menu, high chairs, and the dining room is open to all ages until close. The bar area is 21+.

locations.name: Del Mar

address.street: 2673 Via De La Valle, Suite C

locations.description: Del Mar's flagship bar and dining location — beers on tap, a full bar, and coastal-inspired fish tacos just off Via De La Valle.
locations.seoTitle: Mexican Food in Del Mar — Full Bar & Heated Patio | El Pueblo
locations.seoDescription: Mexican food in Del Mar at 2673 Via De La Valle. Full bar, beers on tap, heated patio, coastal fish tacos. Open 6am–midnight daily. Walking distance to Del Mar Horsepark.

sections.h2: Mexican Food in Del Mar — Full Bar & Coastal Fish Tacos
paragraphs: El Pueblo Del Mar is our flagship bar and dining location — 2673 Via De La Valle, Suite C, minutes from the beach, the racetrack, and the Del Mar Horsepark. This is the El Pueblo you come to when you want the full coastal-Mexican experience: 32 craft beers on tap, a full bar, an outdoor patio, and the fish tacos that made us famous, served alongside a mezcal flight if you're in the mood.

Family-owned, the Del Mar location has been featured in Marketplace, LAist, and the San Diego Union-Tribune for the $1.39 fish taco we've never raised the price on. Locals drop in after surf sessions. Racetrack crowds stop through on their way home. Post-work tables stretch into the evening as the patio fills up.

sections.h2: Full Bar, Outdoor Patio, and Coastal Fish Tacos
paragraphs: Beers on tap cover the essentials and the deep cut — Mexican classics like Pacifico, Modelo, and Victoria, plus San Diego craft staples and seasonal rotations. Beyond the taps, the full bar pours premium tequila, mezcal, house margaritas, and a spicy margarita that Del Mar regulars order by the pitcher.

The patio is the signature room. Outdoor tables, heat lamps for cold nights, and enough space for a private party of 12 without feeling cramped. If you're planning an event — birthday, bachelorette, work dinner, engagement — the Del Mar patio is the one most locals book. Minimum party size for reservations is 8.

sections.h2: Finding Us Off Via De La Valle
paragraphs: We're on Via De La Valle just east of the 5, in the shopping strip at 2673. Easy freeway access, easy parking, a five-minute drive from Del Mar Beach, Torrey Pines State Beach, or the racetrack. Locals from Solana Beach, Rancho Santa Fe, and Carmel Valley come in regularly — we're the halfway point between all three, and the last stop before the coast.

faq.q: Where is El Pueblo Del Mar?
faq.a: 2673 Via De La Valle, Suite C, Del Mar, CA 92014 — just off the 5 freeway on Via De La Valle, minutes from Del Mar Beach and the racetrack.

faq.q: Do you have beer on tap?
faq.a: Yes. Our Del Mar location has a wide selection of beers on tap — a mix of Mexican classics, San Diego craft, and seasonal rotations. Ask for the current tap list at the bar.

faq.q: Is the patio available for private events?
faq.a: Yes. The heated outdoor patio at Del Mar is available for private events starting at 8 guests. We build catering menus around party size and run full bar service during events.

faq.q: Do you take reservations for regular dining?
faq.a: Indoor dining is first-come, first-served. Reservations are only taken for private patio events of 8 or more guests. Walk-ins fill up quickly on race days and summer weekends.

faq.q: Can I watch UFC/PPV fights at El Pueblo Del Mar?
faq.a: Yes. We stream UFC pay-per-views and major fights with no cover — check our News page for upcoming events. The bar opens early on fight nights.

locations.name: La Jolla
locations.description: Our fifth location is coming to La Jolla's Pearl Street in Spring 2026. Sign up below to hear the moment we open the doors.
locations.seoTitle: Mexican Food in La Jolla — Opening Spring 2026 | El Pueblo
locations.seoDescription: Our fifth El Pueblo is coming to La Jolla's Pearl Street in Spring 2026. Fish tacos, breakfast burritos, and the full El Pueblo menu. Sign up for opening-day alerts.

sections.h2: Mexican Food in La Jolla — Coming Spring 2026
paragraphs: El Pueblo's fifth location is coming to La Jolla's Pearl Street in Spring 2026. We're opening at 564 Pearl Street — the former Jack in the Box site — bringing the $1.39 fish taco, breakfast burritos, carne asada, and the full El Pueblo menu to La Jolla Village. The San Diego Union-Tribune covered the news; we've been working toward it since early 2026.

Locations four and a half miles north (Del Mar), ten miles northeast (Carmel Valley), and fourteen miles up the coast (Cardiff and Carlsbad) are open now. La Jolla joins them as the southernmost El Pueblo, within walking distance of La Jolla Cove, Scripps Park, and the shops along Prospect and Pearl.

sections.h2: What to Expect When We Open
paragraphs: The full El Pueblo menu — fish tacos, breakfast burritos, carne asada plates, enchiladas, quesadillas, chile rellenos, tortas, bowls, and kids options — all made fresh every day. Opening hours will run similar to our Del Mar and Carmel Valley kitchens (6am to midnight, seven days a week) pending final permitting. Sign up for the email list below and we'll send one message when we open the doors.

faq.q: When is El Pueblo La Jolla opening?
faq.a: Spring 2026. Exact opening date will be announced on our News page and sent to email subscribers when we have final permitting and a firm launch date.

faq.q: Where will El Pueblo La Jolla be located?
faq.a: 564 Pearl Street, La Jolla, CA 92037 — the former Jack in the Box site, a short walk from La Jolla Cove, Scripps Park, and the shops along Prospect Street.

faq.q: Will the La Jolla menu match the other locations?
faq.a: Yes. La Jolla will serve the full El Pueblo menu — including the $1.39 fish taco, breakfast burritos, carne asada plates, and the kids menu — same recipes, same fresh-every-day kitchen standard.

faq.q: How do I hear when you open?
faq.a: Sign up for our email list on the contact page. We'll send one announcement when La Jolla opens — no spam, no frequent emails.
